page: Amazon Web Services trn1.32xlarge is an x86_64 architecture server powered by dedicated Intel Xeon 8375C processors. It features 128 virtual CPUs across 64 physical cores with a base clock speed of 3.5 GHz and a 54 MB L3 cache. The system is equipped with 512.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core. Storage is supported by 7600 GB of local NVMe SSD capacity, and the instance operates on the Nitro hypervisor with a baseline network bandwidth of 100 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates strong multi-core capabilities, with the Passmark CPU score placing in the top 10% tier at 86301.41 and stress-ng multi-core performance in the top 25% tier. Single-core performance is moderate, with stress-ng single-core and Geekbench single-core scores falling into the middle 50% average tier. Memory subsystem performance is robust, showing strong read-only small block bandwidth of 6667.57 GB/sec and uncached large block write bandwidth of 135.55 GB/sec.

While the server demonstrates strong performance in multi-threaded tasks such as software compilation, database operations, and static web serving, it exhibits limitations in large-scale language model execution. Text generation for a 70B LLM model is weak, registering at 2.78 tokens per second, whereas prompt processing for smaller 135M models is strong at 2985.55 tokens per second. Given the high density of local NVMe SSD storage, dedicated high-vCPU compute, and 100 Gbps network throughput, this server is configured for data-intensive workloads, multi-threaded database operations, and high-throughput web serving.
description: Amazon Web Services trn1.32xlarge is a dedicated x86_64 server featuring 128 vCPUs powered by Intel Xeon 8375C processors running at 3.5 GHz. It includes 512.0 GB of DDR4 memory, 7600 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, and a 100 Gbps baseline network interface managed by the Nitro hypervisor. Benchmarks show top-tier multi-core performance, with a Passmark CPU score in the top 10% and strong memory bandwidth. However, single-core metrics remain average, and text generation for large 70B LLM models is weak at 2.78 tokens per second. This hardware profile is suited for multi-threaded database operations, high-throughput static web serving, and data-intensive processing workloads.
og_description: Amazon Web Services trn1.32xlarge is a dedicated server with 128 vCPUs, 512 GB DDR4 RAM, 7600 GB NVMe SSD storage, and 100 Gbps networking, delivering top-tier multi-core CPU and database performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services trn1.32xlarge features 128 vCPUs, 512 GB RAM, 7.6 TB NVMe SSD, and 100 Gbps networking for high-throughput database workloads.
tagline: A high-capacity x86 server combining 128 dedicated vCPUs, 7.6 TB of local NVMe storage, and 100 Gbps network throughput.
bullet_points: 128 dedicated vCPUs and 64 physical cores via Intel Xeon 8375C processors
512 GB of DDR4 system memory paired with 7600 GB of local NVMe SSD storage
100 Gbps baseline network bandwidth running on the Nitro hypervisor
Top-tier multi-core performance with Passmark CPU score in the top 10%
Suited for high-throughput database operations and multi-threaded data processing
